This check is typically inserted in the preheader of the loop being unrolled. In this case, the preheader of `loop.2` is the block `%if`. This block is also the exit block of `loop.1`.

The verifier error `Instruction does not dominate all uses!` for a use of an LCSSA phi node usually means the use is not in a block dominated by the single exiting block of the loop. In this case, `%load.lcssa` is a PHI in block `%if` (the exit block of `%loop.1`). Any instruction using `%load.lcssa` must be in a block dominated by `%if`.

A similar issue was fixed in the past (D111430) by changing the insertion point for the SCEV expansion. The fix involved ensuring that the expansion happens in the loop's preheader, not before it.

`SCEVExpander Expander(SE, L.getHeader()->getModule()->getDataLayout(), "trip-count");`
`Instruction *TripCountInsertPt = L.getLoopPreheader()->getTerminator();`
`TC = Expander.expandCodeFor(TripCount, TripCount->getType(), TripCountInsertPt);`

`Instruction does not dominate all uses!`
`%load.lcssa = phi i64 [ %load, %loop.1 ]`

Looking at `SCEVExpander::expandCodeFor`, it can insert instructions at the provided insertion point, but it can also try to hoist computations to outer loops if it's safe. The problem is that `%load` is variant in `loop.1`, so hoisting computations involving it outside of `loop.1`'s context is incorrect. The fix is likely to pass the loop preheader as the `PostDominator` argument to `expandCodeFor`. This will ensure that any generated code (including code for sub-expressions of the trip count) is correctly placed in a location that is dominated by any necessary LCSSA phi nodes, which are themselves in the preheader.


`Value *expandCodeFor(const SCEV *Expr, Type *Ty, Instruction *InsertPt, BasicBlock *PostDom = nullptr, const SCEV *Parent = nullptr);`

The logic is that by providing `L.getLoopPreheader()` as the `PostDom` argument, we are telling the `SCEVExpander` that the expanded code will be used in a context that is post-dominated by the preheader. The preheader (`%if` in the example) is where the LCSSA phi for `%load` is located, so any use must be dominated by it. The runtime unrolling logic is inserted in the preheader, so this constraint is correct.

I will apply this change.


  explicit SCEVOperand(unsigned Opc, int Idx, const SCEV *S) :

  /// LLVM instruction opcode that uses the operand.
  unsigned ParentOpcode;

  int OperandIdx;

  const SCEV* S;

};

/// Given an llvm.loop loop id metadata node, returns the loop hint metadata
/// node with the given name (for example, "llvm.loop.unroll.count"). If no
/// such metadata node exists, then nullptr is returned.
MDNode *llvm::GetUnrollMetadata(MDNode *LoopID, StringRef Name) {
  // First operand should refer to the loop id itself.
  assert(LoopID->getNumOperands() > 0 && "requires at least one operand");
  assert(LoopID->getOperand(0) == LoopID && "invalid loop id");

  for (const MDOperand &MDO : llvm::drop_begin(LoopID->operands())) {
    MDNode *MD = dyn_cast<MDNode>(MDO);
    if (!MD)
      continue;

    MDString *S = dyn_cast<MDString>(MD->getOperand(0));
    if (!S)
      continue;

    if (Name == S->getString())
      return MD;
  }
  return nullptr;
}
